source: IRS Rev. Proc. 2024-40 (federal indexed amounts); state DOR sites for state parameters
notes: TCJA estate/gift exemption remains active in 2026 per legislative continuation. No sunset modeled. All values are final published amounts.

annual_exclusion_per_donee.description: Annual gift tax exclusion per donee for 2025.

gift_splitting.description: Spouses may elect to split gifts, doubling the effective annual exclusion.

five_year_election_529.description: Donor may elect to treat a 529 contribution as made ratably over 5 years.

marital_deduction_unlimited.description: Unlimited gift tax marital deduction for transfers to U.S.-citizen spouse.

lifetime_exemption_basic_exclusion.description: Basic exclusion amount for gift and estate tax (2025).

portability_dsue.description: Deceased spousal unused exclusion portable to surviving spouse via timely 706 filing.

marital_deduction.description: Unlimited estate tax marital deduction for transfers to U.S.-citizen surviving spouse.

charitable_deduction.description: Unlimited estate tax deduction for transfers to qualified charities.

administration_debts_deduction.description: Deduction for funeral expenses, administration costs, claims, and debts of decedent.

rate_schedule.description: Federal unified estate/gift transfer tax rate schedule. Top marginal rate 40% above $1M of taxable transfers (effectively above the exemption).

step_up_in_basis.description: Property acquired from a decedent takes a basis equal to fair market value at date of death (or alternate valuation date).

community_property_double_step_up.description: In community property states, both halves of community property receive a step-up at the death of the first spouse.

ird_no_step_up.description: Items of income in respect of a decedent (e.g., traditional IRA, deferred comp) do not receive §1014 step-up.

qdot_non_citizen_spouse.description: Qualified Domestic Trust required for marital deduction when surviving spouse is non-U.S. citizen. Out of MVP scope; schema reserved.

exemption.description: GST exemption equal to estate/gift basic exclusion for 2025.

rate.description: Maximum federal estate tax rate applied to taxable generation-skipping transfers.

skip_person_definition.description: A skip person is a beneficiary two or more generations below the transferor (e.g., grandchildren).

capital_gains_2025_long_term.description: Long-term capital gains and qualified dividend rates for 2025.

niit.description: Net Investment Income Tax 3.8% on investment income above MAGI thresholds. Thresholds are not indexed for inflation.

kiddie_tax_2025.description: First $1,350 of child unearned income is exempt (standard deduction for unearned income). Next $1,350 taxed at child's rate. Unearned income above $2,700 taxed at parent's marginal rate. Applies to children under 19 (or under 24 if full-time student) per §1(g)(2).

ira_contribution_limit.description: Annual IRA contribution limit (under age 50) for 2025.

ira_earned_income_requirement.description: IRA contributions limited to lesser of contribution limit or earned income (compensation).

roth_distribution_qualification.description: Qualified Roth distributions (5-year holding period + age 59½, death, disability, or first-time home purchase) are tax-free.

secure_act_10_year_rule.citation: IRC §401(a)(9)(H) as amended by SECURE Act of 2019
secure_act_10_year_rule.description: Non-spouse beneficiaries of an IRA must distribute the entire account within 10 years of the original owner's death (with limited eligible designated beneficiary exceptions).

secure_2_529_to_roth_rollover.description: Beginning 2024, up to $35,000 lifetime may be rolled from a 529 to a Roth IRA for the designated beneficiary, subject to annual IRA contribution limits, 15-year account age, and other conditions.

tax_free_growth_and_qualified_distributi.description: Earnings grow federal-income-tax-free; qualified higher-education and certain K-12 distributions are tax-free.

nonqualified_withdrawal_penalty.description: 10% additional federal tax on the earnings portion of non-qualified withdrawals, plus ordinary income tax on earnings.

estate_exclusion.description: Donor retains control but assets are excluded from donor's gross estate (except 5-year election proration on death within 5 years).

adequate_safeguards_required.description: A program is a qualified tuition program only if it maintains adequate safeguards against contributions exceeding the amount necessary to provide for the beneficiary's qualified higher education expenses. Implemented by every state via an aggregate per-beneficiary contribution limit.

default_contribution_age_cutoff.description: Engine default: 529 contributions stop at the end of the year the beneficiary turns 22 (typical undergrad completion). This is a planning assumption layered on top of §529(b)(7); it is not itself a federal rule. SECURE 2.0 §126 Roth-rollover ($35K lifetime, 15-yr account age) handles residual corpus past this point.

gift_completed_at_contribution.description: Transfer to a UGMA/UTMA account is a completed gift to the minor for federal gift tax purposes.

custodian_donor_estate_inclusion.description: If the donor serves as custodian and dies before the minor reaches majority, the custodial property is includible in the donor's gross estate.

basis_rules.description: Recipient takes carryover basis on the gift; assets receive §1014 step-up only at the recipient's death (not the donor's).

trump_account.description: Trump Account / 'MAGA Account' established by the One Big Beautiful Bill Act of 2025 for U.S.-citizen children. Tax-deferred custodial account restricted to broad-based U.S. equity index funds. Treasury implementing regulations were still being issued as of the modeled year; parameters below reflect statute as enacted with conservative assumptions on open questions.

federal_seed_amount.description: One-time federal seed deposit for U.S.-citizen children born 2025-2028 (pilot window).

annual_contribution_cap_parent.description: Maximum aggregate annual after-tax contribution from parents and other private sources per beneficiary. Not indexed for inflation as enacted.

annual_contribution_cap_employer_within_.description: Employer contributions are permitted up to $2,500/yr and count against the $5,000 parent cap.

contribution_deductibility.description: Parent contributions are made with after-tax dollars; not deductible at federal level.

investment_restriction.description: Funds restricted to broad-based U.S. equity index investments. Modeled with the same default return as taxable brokerage; no asset-class adjustment in MVP.

early_withdrawal_age.description: Beneficiary may not access funds before age 18. Withdrawals before 18 are not permitted absent narrow hardship rules to be defined by Treasury.

distribution_tax_treatment.citation: OBBBA §70404(g); cross-reference to IRC §72 ordinary-income mechanics
distribution_tax_treatment.description: Distributions after age 18 taxed as ordinary income on the earnings portion (mirroring traditional IRA mechanics). Pending Treasury guidance, the MVP treats the entire balance as taxable ordinary income at the beneficiary's marginal rate when distributed.

rollover_to_ira_available.description: After age 18, account may be rolled into a traditional or Roth IRA subject to the receiving account's contribution rules. MVP models terminal value before rollover.

estate_treatment.description: Parent contributions are completed gifts to the child (§2511); the federal $1,000 seed is a government program transfer (not a gift from the donor). Asset is not includible in donor's gross estate.

kiddie_tax_application.description: Internal account earnings are tax-deferred; kiddie tax (§1(g)) does not apply during the deferral period.

section_2518_disclaimer.description: Qualified disclaimer treated as if disclaimant predeceased decedent. Useful as post-death optimization. Out of MVP active modeling; surfaced as toggle.

section_6166.description: Deferred installment payment of estate tax attributable to closely-held business interests with reduced interest rate. Schema reserved; not active in MVP.

valuation_discounts.description: Special valuation rules for closely-held entity interests. Schema reserved; not active in MVP.

income_tax.description: Graduated state income tax. NYC residents also pay city income tax (modeled as a sub-flag).

nyc_income_tax_top_rate.description: Top NYC resident income tax marginal rate. Applied as a flat add-on at the top bracket for MVP simplicity; structure supports full bracket schedule.

section_529_deduction.citation: NY Tax Law §612(c)(32); NY 529 Direct Plan rules
section_529_deduction.description: Up to $5,000 ($10,000 MFJ) annual deduction for contributions to NY 529 Direct Plan.

section_529_aggregate_contribution_limit.citation: NY 529 Direct Plan Disclosure Booklet; implements IRC §529(b)(7) safeguards
section_529_aggregate_contribution_limit.description: Aggregate per-beneficiary contribution limit for NY 529 plans (combined across all NY 529 accounts for the same beneficiary).

estate_tax.citation: NY Tax Law §§951, 952, 954, 955; NYSDTF Form ET-706
estate_tax.description: $7.16M exemption (2025). If NY taxable estate exceeds 105% of the basic exclusion amount, the entire estate is taxed with NO exemption credit (the 'cliff').

three_year_gift_addback.description: Gifts made within three years of death are added back to the NY gross estate.

section_529_deduction.citation: 35 ILCS 5/203(a)(2)(Y); Bright Start / Bright Directions plan rules
section_529_deduction.description: Up to $10,000 ($20,000 MFJ) annual deduction for contributions to Bright Start or Bright Directions 529 plans.

section_529_aggregate_contribution_limit.citation: Bright Start / Bright Directions Program Disclosure Statement; implements IRC §529(b)(7) safeguards
section_529_aggregate_contribution_limit.description: Aggregate per-beneficiary contribution limit for IL 529 plans (combined across all IL 529 accounts for the same beneficiary).

estate_tax.description: $4M exemption, no portability between spouses, not inflation-indexed. Uses the federal pre-EGTRRA state death tax credit table to compute base tax, then adjusts via a recapture calculation. For MVP, modeled with a simplified graduated approximation that materially tracks the published Illinois Estate Tax Calculator within ~1%.

income_tax.description: Texas does not impose an individual income tax.

section_529_deduction.description: No state income tax means no 529 contribution deduction.

section_529_aggregate_contribution_limit.citation: Texas College Savings Plan / LoneStar 529 Disclosure Statement; implements IRC §529(b)(7) safeguards
section_529_aggregate_contribution_limit.description: Aggregate per-beneficiary contribution limit for Texas-administered 529 plans.

estate_tax.citation: Tex. Tax Code Title 2, ch. 211 (repealed effective 2015)
estate_tax.description: Texas does not impose a state estate or inheritance tax.

defaults.description: Editable defaults applied when user does not supply overrides.
